text: This is a tumor from the front or in coronal section.
The finding at the time of surgery, the surgery was performed under high magnification and illumination of neurosurgical microscope.
You can see this firm tumor and you can see this whitish.

cable which is nothing but the spinal cord and this part of the tumor was pressing on the spinal cord and i am slowly separating it from the spinal cord so that the pressure is gone so this is the firm tumor that we can see this is the

bipolar coagulator which reduces the vascularity and shrinks the size of the tumor so that it becomes manageable for further resection and slowly the spinal cord which is encased in a white tube called urameter is in view and the deeper part of the tumor was fairly firm it required to be cut with scissors

and now the tumor is getting delivered but some portion of the tumor is still stuck down to the pleura which is the covering of the lung and here you can see the spinal cord which is getting released from the compression of the tumor a very very large spinal tumor so you can see this portion of the spinal cord and if you see carefully this tumor is moving with respiration
expansion of the lung because there is a attachment to the pleura and the spinal cord now is totally decompressed.
The pressure is gone and we are separating the tumor from the pleura.
A firm large tumor, it has got separated and it is being slowly dissected and finally delivered out.
So this is the tumor cavity.

this is the spinal cord now completely free of any compression so we are washing the tumor bed with saline to remove any tumor cells which may be lying there after dissection and of course since this part of the spinal column was involved by the tumor it was necessary
to fix the spinal column that is bony spinal column with the rod and screws.
Here we are covering the area of the tumor with what is called as Surgicillin Gel Foam.
These are absorbable materials and get absorbed within one or two months.
